fact: She will not say the cracker-and-meal sentence again; the saying was a one-time opening and Pace did not give the answer she was waiting for

notes: Vee reaches for the kinder interpretation of Randi's tight expression and convinces herself of it; actual truth is ambiguous — the scene suggests Randi's tightness may not have been simple happiness

fact: Randi kissed Vee on the mouth — soft, unhurried, with a second press — when saying goodbye
notes: Direct experience; unambiguous in the text

fact: Vee believes the kiss was a French-style social farewell gesture, not a romantic or sexual act
notes: This is Vee's own rationalization, formed immediately and defensively. The scene's framing — the pause, the second press 'that had something in it the first one hadn't' — strongly implies the rationalization is incorrect. Actual truth marked null as ambiguous but tilting false

fact: Vee is worried that someone in the dorm windows or courtyard may have seen her kiss Randi
notes: Vee actively scans windows and courtyard; the scene does not confirm or deny whether anyone witnessed it

fact: Randi's paper is not due until Thursday, meaning the lie to Brooke was unnecessary in terms of academic urgency
notes: Randi states this directly when Pace asks if she has a paper

fact: Randi eats thin apple slices faster when they are placed cut-side up in the bowl
notes: The narrator clarifies this is something Pace had previously noticed and acted on; confirmed again this scene as he had already prepared the bowl this way. This is prior knowledge made explicit to the reader here, not new to Pace.

fact: When brought to genuine emotional extremity, Randi's voice drops from her trained social register into something lower, broken, and unperformed — her actual voice
notes: Pace had been waiting for this since the first night; this scene is the first time he witnesses it. He explicitly registers the difference.

fact: Randi is capable of a full-body shudder during orgasm — a response he had not seen from her before tonight
notes: Pace observes and explicitly notes this as new

fact: Randi was crying during and after her orgasm, apparently without being aware she was doing it
notes: Pace observes the crying and notes he had not noticed it start; the narrator confirms she did not seem to know she was doing it

fact: When called beautiful by him in an unguarded moment after the scene, Randi flinched involuntarily — suggesting the compliment landed somewhere she was not braced for
notes: Pace feels the flinch physically through her body against his chest and registers it explicitly

fact: Randi locked the bathroom door behind her — something she had never done before in his house
notes: Pace explicitly registers this as a departure from her prior behavior

fact: Randi cried again briefly while alone in the bathroom
notes: Pace hears this while waiting on the bed; he can hear through the door

fact: He believes he has gotten further into Randi tonight than he expected to get this early, and that she will be gradually different from this point forward
notes: Stated as Pace's own internal reflection; the novel's framing does not contradict his assessment

fact: The bench in Pace's bedroom is not a table — it is a purpose-built apparatus designed to restrain and position a woman's body in a specific way
notes: Randi had believed it was a table since her second visit; she revises this belief when she sees it with the wooden top removed and its working surfaces visible

fact: Pace built the bench himself, and began building it eleven months ago — before he met her
notes: Pace tells her directly. The eleven-month timeline is confirmed by the narrator, not by Pace to Randi — she learns only that he was making it before he knew who would be on it.

fact: She once sat on the wooden top of the bench — thinking it was a table — while waiting for Pace
notes: Randi appears to recall this herself and Pace confirms it

fact: She is capable of begging — of asking for something in a voice that comes from genuine need rather than social performance
notes: Randi becomes aware of this about herself during and after the scene; she processes it explicitly in the bathroom mirror sequence

fact: Something has changed in her that she cannot undo — she has crossed a line she cannot uncross
notes: Randi arrives at this belief herself while looking in the bathroom mirror; the novel frames this as genuine self-knowledge rather than distortion

fact: When she tried her practiced knowing smile in the mirror over the face that had just begged, she could see the disjunction between the two — the performance no longer sat cleanly on the face
notes: Randi observes this directly and her eyes fill briefly in response; the smile 'settles' only on a second attempt, and she recognizes it as lying

fact: She does not yet understand what she will do with what happened, or how she feels about Pace for having produced it
notes: Stated as Randi's own interior condition; she explicitly holds both questions open

fact: She is going to have to figure out what she is going to do about Pace — and about what happened tonight
notes: Randi arrives at this conclusion herself in the final paragraphs; framed as a resolve or problem she is holding, not a decision yet made

fact: The Architect (or the man making Vee's dress) measured Vee topless and without her bra
notes: Vee confirms 'The bra too. Yeah.' Randi draws this out through questioning

fact: Vee was physically aroused during the fitting — she experienced visible physical response that she initially attributed to the cold
notes: Vee admits 'actually it was really hot' after Randi's flat repetition of 'Cold' exposes the deflection; Randi appears to have already inferred this

fact: The man making Vee's dress knelt before her on a fitting platform and measured from her left hip
notes: Vee confirms these specific details in response to Randi's leading questions

fact: Vee has a strong emotional and possibly erotic response to being looked at
notes: Randi names this directly and Vee's flustered non-denial confirms it; Vee does not credibly refute it

fact: Vee has a secret about what happened at the moment the dressmaker was kneeling in front of her — something she has told no one, not even Cassie
notes: Vee begins to disclose ('I — There was —') before Randi closes the door with 'You don't have to tell me'; the scene establishes Randi registers that something was there to tell

fact: Vee pays for her own lunch rather than allowing Randi to pay, even when it costs her
notes: Randi witnesses Vee produce her card before Randi can reach for the check; Randi watches the whole transaction
